Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. AyaFlow: A high-performance, eBPF-based network traffic analyzer written in Rust 65 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Kyushu Railway Company Train Varieties 21 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 138 upvotes, 19 comments
  4. Sky Wins Irish Court Order to Unmask 300 Pirate IPTV Users via Revolut Bank 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Show HN: QuickBEAM – run JavaScript as supervised Erlang/OTP processes 52 upvotes, 8 comments
  6. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 92 upvotes, 21 comments
  7. Full network of clitoral nerves mapped out for first time 170 upvotes, 46 comments
  8. More on Version Control 29 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. Netscape News Feed Straight Out of the Late 00s 63 upvotes, 16 comments
  10. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 167 upvotes, 54 comments
  11. The rise and fall of IBM's 4 Pi aerospace computers: an illustrated history 51 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Police used AI facial recognition to wrongly arrest TN woman for crimes in ND 283 upvotes, 109 comments
  13. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 307 upvotes, 120 comments
  14. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics 468 upvotes, 204 comments
  15. Neovim 0.12.0 210 upvotes, 84 comments
  16. Creating West Coast Buddhism (2024) 31 upvotes, 8 comments
  17. Observations from carbon dioxide monitoring 24 upvotes, 6 comments
  18. Ohm's Peg-to-WASM Compiler 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  19. ChatGPT Won't Let You Type Until Cloudflare Reads Your React State 91 upvotes, 40 comments
  20. A nearly perfect USB cable tester 251 upvotes, 135 comments
  21. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 502 upvotes, 301 comments
  22. The Cognitive Dark Forest 85 upvotes, 45 comments
  23. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 28 upvotes, 12 comments
  24. Show HN: BreezePDF – Free, in-browser PDF editor 54 upvotes, 31 comments
  25. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 260 upvotes, 193 comments
  26. C++26 is done ISO C++ standards meeting, Trip Report 114 upvotes, 83 comments
  27. The Epistemology of Microphysics 29 upvotes, 16 comments
  28. My MacBook Keyboard Is Broken and It's Insanely Expensive to Fix 77 upvotes, 80 comments
  29. Show HN: Crazierl – An Erlang Operating System 6 upvotes, 2 comments
  30. Show HN: I made a "programming language" looking for feedback 15 upvotes, 16 comments

Last generated 5:30am, 30 March 2026. Source here.
